named.c(3) Library Functions Manual named.c(3)

NAME

named.c - Named object registry for debugging.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct named_entry
Registry entry for uthash. struct named_registry_t
Global registry with uthash and rwlock. struct named_iter_entry_t

Macros


#define MAX_NAME_LEN 256
#define DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE 768

Typedefs


typedef struct named_entry named_entry_t
Registry entry for uthash.

Functions


asciichat_error_t named_init (void)
void named_destroy (void)
const char * named_register (uintptr_t key, const char *base_name, const char *type, const char *format_spec, const char *file, int line, const char *func, uintptr_t parent_key)
const char * named_register_fmt (uintptr_t key, const char *type, const char *format_spec, const char *file, int line, const char *func, const char *fmt,...)
void named_unregister (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_update_name (uintptr_t key, const char *new_base_name)
const char * named_get (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_get_type (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_get_format_spec (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_describe (uintptr_t key, const char *type_hint)
const char * named_describe_thread (void *thread)
void named_registry_for_each (named_iter_callback_t callback, void *user_data)
const char * named_search_by_type_id (const char *type, void *id)
const char * named_register_fd (int fd, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
const char * named_get_fd (int fd)
const char * named_get_fd_format_spec (int fd)
const char * named_register_packet_type (int pkt_type, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
const char * named_get_packet_type (int pkt_type)
const char * named_get_packet_type_format_spec (int pkt_type)
void named_registry_register_packet_types (void)

Detailed Description

Named object registry for debugging.

Provides a centralized registry for naming any addressable resource. Uses uthash for O(1) lookup by uintptr_t key. Rwlock protects the hash table with minimal critical section.

Definition in file debug/named.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

typedef struct named_entry named_entry_t

Registry entry for uthash.

Function Documentation

const char * named_describe (uintptr_t key, const char * type_hint)

Definition at line 341 of file debug/named.c.

341                                                                  {
342   if (!type_hint) type_hint = "object";
343 
344   static _Thread_local char buffer[DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE];
345 
346   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
347     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint, (ptrdiff_t)key);
348     return buffer;
349   }
350 
351   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
352   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
353   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
354   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
355 
356   if (entry) {
357     const char *type = entry->type ? entry->type : type_hint;
358     const char *name = entry->name ? entry->name : "unknown";
359     if (entry->file && entry->func && entry->line > 0) {
360       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx) @ %s:%d:%s()", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key,
361                entry->file, entry->line, entry->func);
362     } else {
363       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx)", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key);
364     }
365   } else {
366     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint ? type_hint : "unknown", (ptrdiff_t)key);
367   }
368 
369   return buffer;
370 }




References DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE, named_entry::file,
    named_entry::func, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    named_entry::line, named_entry::name, and
    named_entry::type.

const
  char * named_describe_thread (void * thread)


Definition at line 372 of file debug/named.c.


372                                                 {
373   uintptr_t key = asciichat_thread_to_key((asciichat_thread_t)thread);
374   return named_describe(key, "thread");
375 }






References named_describe().






void
  named_destroy (void )


Definition at line 93 of file debug/named.c.


93                          {
94   // Always attempt to shut down the lifecycle, but always cleanup entries
95   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
96 
97   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
98 
99   // Find the registry's own rwlock entry (allocated during named_init bootstrap)
100   // This entry will be unregistered by rwlock_destroy, so we must not free it here
101   uintptr_t registry_lock_key = (uintptr_t)(const void *)&g_named_registry.entries_lock;
102   named_entry_t *registry_lock_entry = NULL;
103   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &registry_lock_key, sizeof(uintptr_t), registry_lock_entry);
104 
105   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL;) {
106     named_entry_t *next = e->hh.next;
107 
108     // Skip the registry lock entry - it will be cleaned up by rwlock_destroy
109     // which calls NAMED_UNREGISTER after we release the lock
110     if (e == registry_lock_entry) {
111       e = next;
112       continue;
113     }
114 
115     // Free all other entries
116     free(e->name);
117     if (e->type) free(e->type);
118     if (e->format_spec) free(e->format_spec);
119     if (e->file) free(e->file);
120     if (e->func) free(e->func);
121     free(e);
122     e = next;
123   }
124 
125   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
126 
127   // rwlock_destroy will call NAMED_UNREGISTER on the lock entry, which will:
128   // 1. Acquire the lock (now safe since we released it)
129   // 2. Find and remove the lock entry from the hash
130   // 3. Free the lock entry's fields
131   rwlock_destroy(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
132 }






References lifecycle_shutdown().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






const
  char * named_get (uintptr_t key)


Definition at line 302 of file debug/named.c.


302                                      {
303   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
304     return NULL;
305   }
306 
307   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
308   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
309   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
310   const char *result = entry ? entry->name : NULL;
311   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
312   return result;
313 }

    named_entry::name.

456   if (fd < 0) return NULL;
457 
458   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
459     return NULL;
460   }
461 
462   uintptr_t key = encode_fd_key(fd);
463   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
464   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
465   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
466   const char *result = entry ? entry->name : NULL;
467   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
468   return result;
469 }

asciichat_error_t
  named_init (void )


Definition at line 74 of file debug/named.c.


74                                    {
75   // Idempotent: if already initialized, return success
76   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
77     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
78   }
79 
80   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_named_registry.lifecycle, "named_registry")) {
81     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
82   }
83 
84   if (rwlock_init(&g_named_registry.entries_lock, "named_registry_lock") != 0) {
85     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
86     return ASCIICHAT_OK;  // Continue even if rwlock init fails
87   }
88 
89   lifecycle_init_commit(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
90   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
91 }






References lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_init_commit(), lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), and rwlock_init().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_init(), and
  main().






const
  char * named_register (uintptr_t key, const char * base_name, const char *
  type, const char * format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func, uintptr_t parent_key)


Definition at line 168 of file debug/named.c.


169                                                                                                {
170 #ifdef NDEBUG
171   // In release builds, skip debug registry and return the base name
172   return base_name ? base_name : "?";
173 #endif
174 
175   if (!base_name || !type || !format_spec) {
176     return "?";
177   }
178 
179   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
180     return base_name;
181   }
182 
183   // If parent_key provided, look up parent name and create hierarchical name
184   char final_base_name[256];
185   if (parent_key != 0) {
186     rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
187     const char *parent_name = named_lookup_name_unlocked(parent_key);
188     rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
189 
190     if (parent_name) {
191       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s#%s", parent_name, base_name);
192     } else {
193       // Parent not found, fall back to base name
194       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
195     }
196   } else {
197     snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
198   }
199 
200   // Generate unique name using atomic counters (only for non-hierarchical names)
201   char name_buffer[256];
202   if (parent_key == 0) {
203     uint64_t counter = 0;
204     if (strcmp(type, "mutex") == 0) {
205       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&mutex_counter, 1);
206     } else if (strcmp(type, "rwlock") == 0) {
207       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&rwlock_counter, 1);
208     } else if (strcmp(type, "cond") == 0) {
209       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&cond_counter, 1);
210     } else if (strcmp(type, "atomic") == 0) {
211       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&atomic_counter, 1);
212     }
213     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s.%"PRIu64, final_base_name, counter);
214   } else {
215     // Hierarchical names don't get counters - parent already has one
216     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s", final_base_name);
217   }
218 
219   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock (avoid long critical section)
220   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
221   if (!entry) return base_name;
222 
223   entry->key = key;
224   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
225   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
226 
227   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
228   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
229   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
230   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
231 
232   return entry->name;
233 }






References named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






const
  char * named_register_fd (int fd, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func)


Definition at line 426 of file debug/named.c.


426                                                                                     {
427   if (fd < 0) {
428     return "?";
429   }
430 
431   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
432     return "?";
433   }
434 
435   char name_buffer[256];
436   snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "fd=%d", fd);
437 
438   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
439   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
440   if (!entry) return "?";
441 
442   uintptr_t key = encode_fd_key(fd);
443   entry->key = key;
444   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
445   named_entry_init(entry, "fd", "%d", file, line, func);
446 
447   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
448   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
449   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
450   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
451 
452   return entry->name;
453 }






References named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






const
  char * named_register_fmt (uintptr_t key, const char * type, const char *
  format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char * func, const char * fmt,
  ...)


Definition at line 235 of file debug/named.c.


236                                                                        {
237   if (!type || !format_spec || !fmt) {
238     return "?";
239   }
240 
241   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
242     return fmt;
243   }
244 
245   // Format the name BEFORE acquiring lock
246   va_list args;
247   va_start(args, fmt);
248   char *full_name = NULL;
249   int ret = vasprintf(&full_name, fmt, args);
250   va_end(args);
251 
252   if (ret < 0) {
253     return "?";
254   }
255 
256   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
257   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
258   if (!entry) {
259     free(full_name);
260     return "?";
261   }
262 
263   // Initialize entry (name is pre-allocated as full_name)
264   entry->key = key;
265   entry->name = full_name;
266   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
267 
268   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
269   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
270   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
271   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
272 
273   return entry->name;
274 }






References args, named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






const
  char * named_register_packet_type (int pkt_type, const char * file, int line,
  const char * func)


Definition at line 487 of file debug/named.c.


487                                                                                                    {
488   if (pkt_type < 0) {
489     return "?";
490   }
491 
492   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
493     return "?";
494   }
495 
496   char name_buffer[256];
497   snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "PACKET_TYPE=%d", pkt_type);
498 
499   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
500   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
501   if (!entry) return "?";
502 
503   uintptr_t key = encode_pkt_type_key(pkt_type);
504   entry->key = key;
505   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
506   named_entry_init(entry, "packet_type", "%d", file, line, func);
507 
508   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
509   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
510   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
511   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
512 
513   return entry->name;
514 }






References named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






void
  named_registry_for_each (named_iter_callback_t callback, void *
  user_data)


Definition at line 382 of file debug/named.c.


382                                                                               {
383   if (!callback || !lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
384     return;
385   }
386 
387   // Snapshot entries while holding read lock
388   named_iter_entry_t entries[256];
389   int count = 0;
390 
391   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
392   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL && count < 256; e = e->hh.next) {
393     entries[count].key = e->key;
394     strncpy(entries[count].name, e->name ? e->name : "?", MAX_NAME_LEN - 1);
395     entries[count].name[MAX_NAME_LEN - 1] = '\0';
396     count++;
397   }
398   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
399 
400   // Call callback outside the lock
401   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
402     callback(entries[i].key, entries[i].name, user_data);
403   }
404 }






References named_iter_entry_t::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), MAX_NAME_LEN, and
    named_iter_entry_t::name.

void
  named_unregister (uintptr_t key)


Definition at line 276 of file debug/named.c.


276                                      {
277   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
278     return;
279   }
280 
281   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
282   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
283   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
284   if (entry) {
285     HASH_DEL(g_named_registry.entries, entry);
286     free(entry->name);
287     if (entry->type) free(entry->type);
288     if (entry->format_spec) free(entry->format_spec);
289     if (entry->file) free(entry->file);
290     if (entry->func) free(entry->func);
291     free(entry);
292   }
293   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
294 }

    named_entry::format_spec, named_entry::func,
